text: The two-stage time series design has emerged as a cornerstone of analytical methodology in environmental epidemiology. Recent advancements have significantly enhanced both stages of this framework: distributed lag non-linear models (DLNMs) now allow for the simultaneous exploration of non-linear effects and delayed temporal dependencies, while multivariate meta-analysis provides a robust mechanism for pooling complex, multi-parameter associations across different locations. Despite these methodological breakthroughs, the integrated application of these two techniques remains infrequent and lacks a standardized procedural approach.
